zh.lifeSummary: 當Captain William John Collins Lewis於1603年8月10日出生在英國素可易亞斯特布恩時，他的父親Edward Lewis35歲，母親Mary31歲。他於1621年與Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead在英國英格蘭素可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1657年9月16日在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可馬爾登享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Lewis, Leweis, Lowis, from ancient Germanic (originally West Frankish) Hludwig (itself from hlōd- ‘fame, famous’ + wīg- ‘battle’). This was Latinized as Ludovicus and Chlodovisus, which were gallicized as Clovis or Clouis, French Louis. The name may also appear as Lawis, Laweys, Lawes, by unrounding of the vowel of Lowis on the analogy of the variation between Low and Law as pet forms of Middle English Lourence alias Laurence. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Laws 2 and compare Lawrence .
Welsh: adopted for the Welsh personal name Llywelyn (see Llewellyn ).

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’. This is one of the most common Old Irish personal names. It is derived from Lugh ‘brightness’, which was the name of a Celtic god.

variants.name: Laws
meanings: English: variant of Law (pet form of Lawrence ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Perhaps a shortened form of Scottish or Irish   McLeish  . Compare   McLaws  .

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: McGlew
meanings: Irish: most probably from Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh’, a Gaelic personal name (referencing the god Lugh), sometimes Anglicized as Lewis . However, both Woulfe and MacLysaght consider McGlew to be a shortened form of Mac Dhuinnshléibhe (see McAleavey and Dunleavy ).

nameMeanings.name: Captain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Constant, Emile, Marcell.

Americanized form (translation into English) of French Capitaine, Dutch Captein or Kaptein , Italian Capitano , German and Polish or other Slavic Kapitan , Hungarian Kapitány, and Greek Kapetanos, all status names from a title meaning ‘captain’ (ultimately from Late Latin capitaneus ‘chief’, ‘principal’ from caput ‘head’), used to denote a master of a ship, a high-ranking official, a headman, or a person of military rank. In North America, the surname Captain may also be derived (shortened and translated) from various Greek patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Kapetanopoulos. Compare Capitan .

variants.name: Capitano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Sal, Angelo, Gilda, Orazio, Sarina.
Italian (Sicily and Naples): status name from capitano ‘captain’, a title denoting the master of a ship, an official of some kind, or an officer in the army. Compare Captain .

variants.name: Kapitan
meanings: From Late Latin Capito, originally a nickname meaning ‘big-headed’; the name of a missionary who preached in the Crimea and south Russia in the 4th century.

variants.name: Kaptein
meanings: Dutch: status name from kaptein, a title meaning ‘captain’. Compare Captain .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Captain William John Collins Lewis was born on 10 August 1603, in Eastbourne, Sussex, England, his father, Edward Lewis, was 35 and his mother, Mary, was 31. He married Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead in 1621, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 16 September 1657, in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 54.

Il a épousé Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead en 1621, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 16 septembre 1657, à Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 54 ans.

Lui sposato Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead nel 1621, nel Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. ko.lifeSummary: Captain William John Collins Lewis(이)가 1603년 8월 10일 영국 수세스 이스트보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Lewis(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1621년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1657년 9월 16일 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 맬든에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Captain William John Collins Lewis родился 10 августа 1603 г. в местности эастбурн, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Lewis было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Hanhah Stole Mead в 1621 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 16 сентября 1657 г. в местности малден, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 54 лет.
